Title: On-Set Polish & Editorial Round-Trip
Description: Sprint 13 polishes the Tauri on-set app to production-ready quality: 72-hour offline simulation test, Basecamp self-hosted mode, day-end turnover PDF generation, conflict resolution UI, and Loro CRDT persistence for continuity notes on-set. Sprint 14 delivers the full editorial round-trip: OTIO and AAF export from the Script AST (with scriptos.* custom metadata), correlation DB, EDL export, format normaliser (AAF/EDL → OTIO via Python subprocess), diff engine (omissions, reorders, take changes, untracked clips), editorial re-import Temporal saga, and Frame.io OAuth integration with webhook comment linkage. Together these two sprints complete the production-to-post handoff loop and prepare ScriptOS for GA. Reference: Wiki/15-roadmap.md Sprints 13 and 14, Wiki/09-post-production.md, ADR-007, ADR-022, ADR-023.
Acceptance Criteria:
- 72-hour offline test: 300 takes, 100 assertions, 50 photos — all sync correctly on reconnect
- Basecamp mode: 3 Tauri clients sync via local WiFi (no internet) to basecamp laptop
- Day-end turnover PDF generated for a test shooting day
- Tauri app auto-updates to new version via Tauri updater
- OTIO export: clips have scriptos.scene_id in custom metadata
- AAF export: SCRIPTOS_SCENE_ID in Avid SourceMob custom data
- Editorial diff detects omissions, reorders, take changes, and untracked clips correctly
- Correlation DB fallback matching: correct scene IDs resolved after stripping OTIO metadata
- Editorial re-import saga: approved operations applied to script graph
- Frame.io comment received and linked to take within 5s of webhook delivery
Tags: on-set; tauri; editorial; otio; aaf; frame-io; sprint-13; sprint-14

Title: Tauri On-Set App Polish and Basecamp Mode
Description: Production-harden the Tauri on-set app for real shooting days: 72-hour offline simulation test, Basecamp self-hosted PowerSync Docker Compose stack, day-end turnover package generation, Loro CRDT for collaborative continuity notes on local WiFi, conflict resolution UI for take disagreements, photo gallery, and Tauri auto-updater. Reference: Wiki/06-offline-sync.md, Wiki/15-roadmap.md Sprint 13.
Acceptance Criteria:
- 72-hour offline simulation: 300 takes, 100 assertions, 50 photos — all sync correctly
- Basecamp mode: 3 Tauri clients sync via local WiFi without internet
- Day-end turnover PDF generated and saved to Desktop from Tauri
- Tauri auto-update downloads and applies on launch
- Conflict resolution UI: two circled takes flagged; supervisor resolves; resolution syncs

Title: Editorial Export — OTIO AAF EDL and Correlation DB
Description: Export the Script AST to OTIO, AAF (Avid), and EDL (CMX 3600) formats with scriptos.* custom metadata in each. Populate the correlation DB on export. Build the format normaliser (AAF/EDL → OTIO via Python subprocess) and the diff engine (detect omissions, reorders, take changes, untracked clips). Implement the editorial re-import Temporal saga. Reference: Wiki/09-post-production.md, ADR-007, ADR-022, Wiki/15-roadmap.md Sprint 14.
Acceptance Criteria:
- OTIO export: clips contain scriptos.scene_id and scriptos.take_id in custom metadata
- AAF export: SCRIPTOS_SCENE_ID in Avid SourceMob custom data; valid AAF file importable in Media Composer
- Correlation DB: one record per scene per export; fallback matching resolves scene IDs without metadata
- Diff engine: detects omissions, reorders, take changes, untracked clips correctly on test cases
- Editorial re-import saga: approved operations applied to script graph (scene status updated)
Tags: editorial; otio; aaf; edl; correlation; sprint-14

Title: Frame.io Integration — OAuth and Webhook Comment Linkage
Description: Connect Frame.io to ScriptOS via OAuth 2.0. Receive Frame.io comment.created webhooks; link comments to ScriptOS takes by timecode + correlation metadata. Display review comments in the web app linked to the relevant take. Reference: ADR-023, Wiki/09-post-production.md, Wiki/15-roadmap.md Sprint 14.
Acceptance Criteria:
- OAuth flow: 'Connect Frame.io' button → OAuth → frameio_credentials stored → integration shows as active
- Webhook: comment.created from Frame.io → ReviewComment stored in DB within 5s
- Review comment linked to correct take by timecode
- Review comments panel shows: timecode, reviewer name, comment text, take reference

Title: As a script supervisor the Tauri on-set app works reliably for a full 12-plus-hour shooting day and the Basecamp offline mode keeps an entire unit working without internet
Description: The most demanding real-world scenario is a 12-16 hour shooting day in a location with no internet. The app must not lose data, must allow full continuity workflow, and must give the supervisor confidence that everything will sync when connectivity returns. Basecamp mode extends this to an entire unit on a local network. Reference: Wiki/06-offline-sync.md.
Acceptance Criteria:
- Automated 72-hour offline test: simulate 300 takes, 100 assertions, 50 photo uploads; on reconnect all data appears in cloud Postgres
- Basecamp Docker Compose: `docker compose -f docker-compose.basecamp.yml up` starts local PowerSync + Postgres on a laptop
- 3 Tauri clients connect to basecamp laptop via LAN and sync in real-time
- Basecamp setup script: dumps production project data for offline use
- App auto-update: new version downloaded and applied on launch; no manual install required
Parent: Tauri On-Set App Polish and Basecamp Mode

Title: As a script supervisor I can generate a day-end turnover package from the Tauri app and collaborate on continuity notes in real-time with another supervisor via local WiFi
Description: Day-end turnover is a legally and contractually required deliverable — the lined script, take log, and deviation report must be handed to the production office before wrapping. Collaborative continuity notes on-set enable two supervisors (one per camera) to share context in real-time on local WiFi without depending on cloud connectivity. Reference: Wiki/06-offline-sync.md.
Acceptance Criteria:
- Day-end turnover PDF: contains lined script pages (scene headings + circle/print/no-print indicators), take log (scene, take number, rating, notes), deviation report (all logged deviations grouped by scene)
- PDF saved to macOS Desktop via Tauri file system API
- Two Tauri devices on same LAN WiFi share continuity note edits in real-time via Loro CRDT over local WebSocket
- Continuity note CRDT state persists in SQLite across Tauri app restarts
Parent: Tauri On-Set App Polish and Basecamp Mode

Title: As a post-production supervisor I can export the Script AST to OTIO AAF and EDL formats and re-import NLE changes with a diff-reviewed approval workflow
Description: The editorial round-trip is one of the highest-value features for professional productions — it eliminates manual script supervisor notes cross-referencing with editorial. Correlation metadata in every clip ensures changes can be traced back to the correct scene even if the NLE editor has renamed or re-ordered clips. Reference: Wiki/09-post-production.md, ADR-007, ADR-022.
Acceptance Criteria:
- OTIO export button in editorial export modal; OTIO file downloads within 5s for 100-scene script
- AAF export: file importable in Avid Media Composer without errors; custom data visible in SourceMob
- EDL export: reel names ≤ 8 chars; valid CMX 3600 format
- Correlation DB populated on export: one record per scene with scene_id, version_id, export_batch_id
- Re-import: drag-and-drop OTIO/AAF triggers editorial re-import saga; diff shown in change review UI
- Approved operations applied: omitted scene status set to 'omitted'; reordered scene position updated
Tags: editorial; otio; aaf; edl; post-production; sprint-14
Parent: Editorial Export — OTIO AAF EDL and Correlation DB

Title: As a DIT or producer I can connect Frame.io to ScriptOS so that review comments from dailies are linked to the correct takes in the supervisor log
Description: Frame.io is the industry standard for dailies review. Linking Frame.io comments directly to supervisor take records eliminates the need for manual note cross-referencing and keeps all review feedback in one place. Reference: ADR-023.
Acceptance Criteria:
- 'Connect Frame.io' button in integration settings opens OAuth flow; on completion shows 'Frame.io connected'
- Incoming comment.created webhook stored as ReviewComment linked to take within 5s
- Comments visible in Review Comments panel: timecode, reviewer name, comment text, take link
- Clicking take link in comments panel navigates to that take in the supervisor log
Parent: Frame.io Integration — OAuth and Webhook Comment Linkage

Title: Implement 72-hour offline simulation test Basecamp Docker Compose and Basecamp setup script
Description: In tests/e2e/offline-72h.test.ts: automated test using Playwright or a custom Tauri test harness. Simulate: (1) Start Tauri app; disable network adapter (macOS NetworkExtension or mock). (2) Run 300 logTake + 100 createContinuityAssertion + 50 attachContinuityPhoto Tauri commands over 72h simulated time (accelerated). (3) Re-enable network. (4) Wait max 120s. (5) Query cloud Postgres; assert all 300 takes, 100 assertions, and 50 photo S3 keys present. In infra/basecamp/docker-compose.basecamp.yml: services: postgres (Cloud SQL image with prod schema), powersync-self-hosted (PowerSync open source Docker image). In scripts/basecamp-setup.sh: pg_dump production project data for one project; apply to local Postgres; start compose stack. Document in docs/basecamp-setup.md. Basecamp LAN test: start 3 Tauri dev instances; point PowerSync endpoint to laptop IP; assert take logged on device A appears on device B within 10s.
Parent: As a script supervisor the Tauri on-set app works reliably for a full 12-plus-hour shooting day

Title: Implement day-end turnover PDF generation from Supervisor Service data and expose via Tauri command
Description: In services/supervisor/src/export/turnover.generator.ts implement generateTurnoverPDF(sessionIds: UUID[]): Promise<Buffer> using PDFKit. Document structure: (1) Header: production name, shoot date, generation timestamp. (2) Lined script section: for each scene session, render scene heading; for each take render row: take number, rating icon (circle/print/no-print/hold), notes text. (3) Deviation report: scenes with deviations grouped; each deviation: type badge (DIALOGUE/ACTION/PROP/WARDROBE), description. (4) Continuity assertions: per scene, list assertions with subject + text + photo thumbnail (if photo_s3_key exists: download thumbnail from S3; embed as JPEG). Format: A4, 12pt Courier for script sections, 10pt Calibri for tables. In Tauri: add Tauri command generate_turnover(shoot_day_id): calls Supervisor Service REST endpoint; receives PDF bytes; writes to ~/Desktop/Turnover_[shootDate].pdf using Tauri fs API. Vitest test: generate turnover for a 3-scene test session; assert PDF is non-empty; assert contains scene headings.
Parent: As a script supervisor I can generate a day-end turnover package from the Tauri app

Title: Implement Loro CRDT SQLite persistence for collaborative continuity notes in Tauri with local WiFi sync
Description: In apps/onset/src-tauri/src/crdt/: implement CrdtStore using Loro Rust bindings (loro-crdt crate). On init: load existing CRDT state from SQLite column crdt_doc_states.data_bytes for each scene_session. On note edit: apply operation to Loro Doc; encode state update; write to SQLite. WebSocket server: start a local WebSocket server (tokio-tungstenite) on port 9090 when app launches. Broadcast CRDT updates to all connected local clients (other Tauri instances on same WiFi). On incoming update: apply to local Loro Doc; re-render React UI (Tauri event emit). React: ContinuityNotes.tsx component; textarea backed by Loro text CRDT; renders updates from remote clients in real-time. Integration test: start 2 Tauri dev instances; type 'Hello' on instance A; assert 'Hello' appears on instance B within 1s.
Parent: As a script supervisor I can generate a day-end turnover package from the Tauri app and collaborate on continuity notes

Title: Create Atlas DDL for editorial.export_batches correlation_records reimport_batches and implement OTIO export from Script AST
Description: In packages/db/schema/editorial.hcl: (1) editorial.export_batches: id UUID PK, script_version_id UUID FK, export_format ENUM(OTIO,AAF,EDL), exported_by UUID, exported_at TIMESTAMPTZ, s3_key TEXT. (2) editorial.correlation_records: id UUID PK, export_batch_id UUID FK, scene_id UUID, take_id UUID, clip_name TEXT, otio_track_name TEXT, otio_range_start FLOAT8, otio_range_duration FLOAT8. (3) editorial.reimport_batches: id UUID PK, source_export_batch_id UUID FK, upload_s3_key TEXT, status ENUM(pending,normalizing,diffing,reviewing,applying,done,failed), created_at TIMESTAMPTZ. In services/supervisor/src/editorial/otio.exporter.ts implement exportToOTIO(scriptVersionId): Promise<string> (OTIO JSON string). Use otio-python via Python subprocess call (spawn Python script). OTIO structure: one Timeline per script; one Track per episode; one Clip per scene. Custom metadata on each clip: { 'scriptos.scene_id': sceneId, 'scriptos.scene_number': sceneNumber, 'scriptos.circled_take': circledTakeId }. Correlation DB: insert one correlation_records row per scene on export. Integration test: export 5-scene script; parse OTIO JSON; assert 5 clips with correct scriptos.scene_id metadata.
Parent: As a post-production supervisor I can export the Script AST to OTIO AAF and EDL formats

Title: Implement AAF export for Avid Media Composer delivery and EDL export in CMX 3600 format
Description: In services/supervisor/src/editorial/aaf.exporter.ts implement exportToAAF(scriptVersionId): Promise<Buffer>. Use aaf-writer Python library via subprocess. Structure: one Composition per script; one MasterMob per episode; one SourceMob per scene. Custom data on SourceMob: SCRIPTOS_SCENE_ID property (AAF custom data string property). Export format: .aaf binary. In services/supervisor/src/editorial/edl.exporter.ts implement exportToEDL(scriptVersionId): Promise<string>. CMX 3600 format: header line (FCM: NON-DROP FRAME), one event per scene: event number (001-999), reel name (≤ 8 chars: 'SC001001'), track type (V), edit type (C), source in/out timecodes, record in/out timecodes. Timecodes derived from shoot_day + scene_order + estimated scene duration. Integration test for AAF: export and parse with aaf-reader; assert SCRIPTOS_SCENE_ID present. Integration test for EDL: export; assert valid CMX 3600 line format for each event.
Parent: As a post-production supervisor I can export the Script AST to OTIO AAF and EDL formats

Title: Build format normaliser AAF and EDL to OTIO via Python subprocess and implement editorial diff engine
Description: In services/supervisor/src/editorial/normalizer.ts implement normalizeToOTIO(s3Key: string, format: 'aaf'|'edl'): Promise<string> (returns OTIO JSON). Downloads file from S3; spawns Python subprocess: python3 scripts/normalize_to_otio.py --input /tmp/file.aaf --format aaf → writes OTIO JSON to stdout. Python script uses opentimelineio library. In services/supervisor/src/editorial/diff.engine.ts implement diffOTIO(baseOTIOJson, reimportedOTIOJson, correlationRecords): EditorialDiff. EditorialDiff: { omissions: { sceneId, clipName }[], reorders: { sceneId, oldPosition, newPosition }[], takeChanges: { sceneId, oldTakeId, newTakeId }[], untrackedClips: { clipName, timecodeRange }[] }. Algorithm: (1) Build clip-to-sceneId map from correlation records by OTIO metadata lookup. (2) Fallback: if metadata missing, use correlation_records.clip_name + timecode range fuzzy match. (3) Compare clip order between base and reimported. (4) Identify clips present in base but absent in reimport = omissions. (5) Unmatched clips = untrackedClips. Integration test: craft base + modified OTIO with one omission and one reorder; assert diff detects both.
Parent: As a post-production supervisor I can export the Script AST to OTIO AAF and EDL formats

Title: Implement editorial re-import Temporal saga applying approved diff operations to the Script graph
Description: In workers/revision-dist/src/workflows/editorial-reimport.workflow.ts implement EditorialReimportWorkflow. Steps: (1) DownloadAndNormalizeActivity: download from S3; normalizeToOTIO subprocess; return OTIO JSON. (2) DiffActivity: diffOTIO against base export; return EditorialDiff. (3) ReviewGateActivity: create reimport_batches row with status=reviewing; await approveReimport signal (reviewer decision via GraphQL mutation). (4) ApplyOperationsActivity: for each approved operation: Omission → call ScriptService.updateSceneStatus(sceneId, 'omitted'); Reorder → call ScriptService.reorderScene(sceneId, newPosition); TakeChange → call SupervisorService.setCircledTake(sceneId, newTakeId). (5) FinalizeActivity: set reimport_batches.status = done. Expose via GraphQL: mutation startEditorialReimport(reimportBatchId): ID! (starts Temporal workflow). In SupervisorService: implement setCircledTake gRPC method. Integration test: run saga with a 3-operation diff (1 omission, 1 reorder, 1 take change); assert all 3 applied to script after approval signal.
Parent: As a post-production supervisor I can export the Script AST to OTIO AAF and EDL formats

Title: Implement Frame.io OAuth 2.0 integration webhook handler and ReviewComment DB storage
Description: In services/supervisor/src/integrations/frameio/: (1) OAuth flow: GET /frameio/oauth/connect → redirect to Frame.io OAuth authorize URL. GET /frameio/oauth/callback → exchange code for tokens; store in editorial.frameio_credentials (id, org_id, access_token, refresh_token, expires_at, account_id). Atlas DDL: add editorial.frameio_credentials table. (2) Webhook endpoint: POST /frameio/webhook. Verify HMAC-SHA256 signature (X-Frameio-Signature header). Parse payload: event = 'comment.created' → extract { asset_id, timecode, author_name, comment_text }. Resolve asset_id to take_id via correlation_records.clip_name fuzzy match + timecode range intersection. Insert editorial.review_comments row: { take_id, frameio_asset_id, timecode, author_name, comment_text, received_at }. Respond 200. (3) GraphQL subgraph extension: extend type Take { reviewComments: [ReviewComment!]! }. type ReviewComment { id, timecode, authorName, commentText, receivedAt }. Integration test: POST mock webhook payload; assert ReviewComment inserted; assert take_id matched within 5s.
Parent: As a DIT or producer I can connect Frame.io to ScriptOS so that review comments from dailies are linked to the correct takes

Title: Build editorial export modal re-import upload change review UI and Frame.io connect settings in web app
Description: Create apps/web/src/editorial/components/EditorialExportModal.tsx: opened from script detail page via 'Export to Editorial' button. Three format checkboxes: OTIO, AAF, EDL. Download button: calls exportToOTIO/exportToAAF/exportToEDL REST endpoints; downloads file. Create EditorialReimportPanel.tsx: drag-and-drop zone accepting .otio and .aaf files; on drop: upload to S3 presigned URL; call startEditorialReimport mutation; redirect to ChangeReviewPage. ChangeReviewPage: shows editorial diff operations in a table (type: omission/reorder/take-change/untracked; scene number; description); per-row approve/reject checkbox; 'Approve Selected' button calls approveEditorialOperations mutation. UntrakcedClipQueue: lists untracked clips; manual scene mapping dropdown. Create ReviewCommentsPanel.tsx in editor right sidebar: shows reviewComments for selected take; each comment shows author icon, timecode badge, comment text, timestamp. Vitest test: render ChangeReviewPage with 3 diff operations; select 2; click Approve; assert mutation called with 2 operation IDs.
Parent: As a post-production supervisor I can export the Script AST to OTIO AAF and EDL formats
